title: Setaria scheelei
taxonomy: Setaria scheelei is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Setaria, which is a group of annual or perennial grasses commonly known as bristle grasses. Setaria scheelei is classified as a member of the tribe Paniceae, which includes other grasses such as oats and wild rye. The species is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Setaria scheelei is often found growing in areas with full sun and well-drained soil.

intro: Setaria scheelei, also known as the woolly foxtail or woolly grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has identified several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Setaria scheelei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
